root_text.sn23.9:1.2: Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho āyasmantaṁ rādhaṁ bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.sn23.9:1.3: “rūpe kho, rādha, yo chando yo rāgo yā nandī yā taṇhā, taṁ pajahatha.
root_text.sn23.9:1.4: Evaṁ taṁ rūpaṁ pahīnaṁ bhavissati ucchinnamūlaṁ tālāvatthukataṁ anabhāvaṅkataṁ āyatiṁ anuppādadhammaṁ.
root_text.sn23.9:1.5: Vedanāya yo chando yo rāgo yā nandī yā taṇhā, taṁ pajahatha.
root_text.sn23.9:1.6: Evaṁ sā vedanā pahīnā bhavissati ucchinnamūlā tālāvatthukatā anabhāvaṅkatā āyatiṁ anuppādadhammā.
root_text.sn23.9:1.8: saṅkhāresu yo chando yo rāgo yā nandī yā taṇhā, taṁ pajahatha.
root_text.sn23.9:1.9: Evaṁ te saṅkhārā pahīnā bhavissanti ucchinnamūlā tālāvatthukatā anabhāvaṅkatā āyatiṁ anuppādadhammā.
root_text.sn23.9:1.10: Viññāṇe yo chando yo rāgo yā nandī yā taṇhā, taṁ pajahatha.
root_text.sn23.9:1.11: Evaṁ taṁ viññāṇaṁ pahīnaṁ bhavissati …pe… anuppādadhamman”ti.

translation_text.sn23.9:1.2: When Venerable Rādha was seated to one side, the Buddha said to him:
translation_text.sn23.9:1.3: “Rādha, you should give up any desire, greed, relishing, and craving for form.
translation_text.sn23.9:1.4: Thus that form will be given up, cut off at the root, made like a palm stump, obliterated, and unable to arise in the future.
translation_text.sn23.9:1.5: You should give up any desire, greed, relishing, and craving for feeling …
translation_text.sn23.9:1.11: Thus that consciousness will be given up, cut off at the root, made like a palm stump, obliterated, and unable to arise in the future.”
